mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving real activity and earnings revisions; higher INDPRO growth at the decision point predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth does not produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is at recessionary levels, household income risk and earnings downgrades dominate valuation support; UNRATE at or above 9% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near zero after a rate-cutting cycle lowers discount rates and raises the present value of future earnings; FEDFUNDS below 0.25% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing at the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% episodes fail to produce positive excess 60-day S&P 500 returns versus all other episodes, or if the sign of the estimated coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below depression thresholds signals weak household formation, construction demand, and credit availability; HOUST below 600 predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST < 600 observations have higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations in a walk-forward test, or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales acceleration during very high unemployment indicates resilient consumption despite labor stress; the joint condition of rising RSAFS and UNRATE above 9% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE >= 9.0% and RSAFS 3-month percent change crosses above 0
regime_hint: post-shock consumption recovery with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform cases where UNRATE >= 9.0% and RSAFS growth is nonpositive, or if average triggered 20-day returns are <= 0.

mechanism_narrative: Positive CPI growth while FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero creates highly accommodative real monetary conditions; the interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-positive and FEDFUNDS-low interaction has a nonpositive coefficient for 60-day returns, or if triggered returns do not exceed non-triggered returns by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below the 2017 index benchmark signals a near-term output gap and inventory adjustment risk; INDPRO below 100 predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below index level 100 or remains < 100
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO < 100 observations produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or if the triggered mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll contraction combined with a rising unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor income and weaker aggregate demand; falling PAYEMS with rising UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE 3-month change > 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if adding the PAYEMS and UNRATE interaction fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy.
